data.title: U.S. President Donald Trump: No Rush for Agreement; More Targets to Hit
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump stated that he is not in a hurry to reach an agreement, emphasizing that there are more targets to address. According to Jin10, Trump expressed confidence in his approach, suggesting that the current strategy will continue to focus on additional objectives. This statement reflects the administration's ongoing stance on negotiations and strategic priorities.

data.title: Bank of England's Taylor: Low Risk of UK Inflation Unanchoring Due to Weak Labor Market
data.description: The Bank of England's Monetary Policy Committee member, Taylor, has indicated that the risk of UK inflation becoming unanchored is low. According to Jin10, this assessment is based on the current weakening of the labor market and the slowdown in wage growth. Taylor's comments suggest that these economic factors are contributing to a more stable inflation outlook for the UK.

data.title: Bank of England's Taylor: Rate Cuts Possible if Risks Diminish
data.description: Bank of England Monetary Policy Committee member Taylor has indicated that the central bank may consider further interest rate cuts if economic shocks are mild or short-lived. According to Jin10, Taylor emphasized that once the risks subside, there could be room for easing monetary policy. This statement comes amid ongoing discussions about the economic outlook and the potential need for adjustments in response to changing conditions.

data.title: Bank of England Faces Tough Choices Amid Inflation and Economic Growth Concerns
data.description: The Bank of England's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) may encounter more challenging decisions if disruptions persist and shocks intensify, according to MPC member Taylor. According to Jin10, Taylor highlighted the potential dilemma between managing high inflation and addressing sluggish economic growth. The ongoing economic environment presents significant challenges for policymakers as they navigate these complex issues.

data.title: Iran Allows 10 Oil Tankers to Pass Through Strait of Hormuz, U.S. President Trump Says
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump announced that Iran has permitted 10 oil tankers to transit the Strait of Hormuz as a 'gift.' According to Jin10, this development comes amid ongoing tensions in the region, highlighting the strategic importance of the waterway for global oil transportation. The Strait of Hormuz is a critical chokepoint through which a significant portion of the world's oil supply is shipped. This gesture by Iran may be seen as an attempt to ease tensions and ensure the uninterrupted flow of oil, which is vital for the global economy. The move could have implications for international relations and energy markets, as stakeholders closely monitor the situation for any further developments.

data.title: France's Armed Forces Chief Discusses Reopening of Hormuz Strait with Global Counterparts
data.description: France's Ministry of Defense announced that the Chief of Staff of the French Armed Forces held a video conference with representatives from 35 countries across various continents. According to Jin10, the meeting focused on exploring ways to reopen the strategic Hormuz Strait. The discussion comes amid ongoing geopolitical tensions affecting the vital maritime passage, which is crucial for global oil transportation. The conference underscores the international community's interest in ensuring the security and accessibility of this key shipping route.

data.title: Wall Street Backs Firms in Legal Battle Over Alleged Credit Market Cartel
data.description: Wall Street trade groups have expressed support for Apollo and other firms in their efforts to dismiss a lawsuit filed by Optimum. Bloomberg posted on X, the lawsuit accuses the lenders of creating an 'illegal cartel' to exclude the telecom company from the U.S. credit market. The case has drawn significant attention as it involves major financial entities and allegations of anti-competitive practices. The outcome could have implications for the credit market and the legal boundaries of lender collaborations.

data.title: Quebec Legislature Calls for Air Canada CEO's Resignation Over Language Issue
data.description: Quebec's legislature has voted to demand the resignation of Air Canada's CEO following his failure to speak French in a video addressing the recent deadly collision at LaGuardia Airport. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the controversy surrounding the CEO's language choice, which has sparked significant backlash in Quebec, where French is the official language. The incident has intensified discussions about the importance of bilingual communication in Canadian corporations, particularly those operating in regions with strong linguistic identities. The legislature's decision reflects the broader sentiment in Quebec regarding the preservation and promotion of the French language in public and corporate communications.

data.title: U.S. President Donald Trump Jokes About Iran Uranium Seizure
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump held a cabinet meeting today, where he was questioned about the possibility of entering Iran to seize uranium. According to Jin10, Trump declined to provide a direct answer and instead made a lighthearted remark, saying, 'We will act at 3 p.m. tomorrow.'

data.title: U.S. President Donald Trump: Tensions Between Russia and Ukraine Easing
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has expressed his belief that the animosity between Russian President Vladimir Putin and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky is diminishing. According to Jin10, Trump noted that the relationship between the two leaders appears to be improving, suggesting a potential easing of tensions between Russia and Ukraine. This development could have significant implications for the geopolitical landscape in the region, as both countries have been involved in a prolonged conflict. The U.S. President's remarks come amid ongoing international efforts to mediate and resolve the disputes between the two nations.

data.title: UAE Developers Address Investor Concerns Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: Developers in the United Arab Emirates have engaged in discussions with investors to mitigate worries about a possible liquidity crisis. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the shift in circumstances as the conflict involving Iran nears its one-month duration. The developers aim to reassure stakeholders and maintain stability in the face of geopolitical tensions impacting the region's economic landscape.

data.title: U.S. President Donald Trump Considered Halting Federal Gasoline Tax
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has revealed that he once contemplated suspending the federal gasoline tax. According to Jin10, this consideration was part of broader discussions on economic measures. The federal gasoline tax is a significant component of fuel pricing in the United States, and any changes to it could have substantial implications for both consumers and the economy. The idea reflects ongoing debates about how best to manage economic pressures and support American households. However, no official decision or policy change has been announced regarding this matter.

data.title: USDC Treasury Mints 250 Million USDC
data.description: According to NS3.AI, Whale Alert has reported that the USDC Treasury has minted 250 million USDC. This development is part of the ongoing activities within the cryptocurrency market, reflecting the dynamics of stablecoin issuance and its potential impact on market liquidity.

data.title: Greeks.live: Tomorrow will see the largest options settlement in Q1, with nearly 40% of options expiring.
data.description: Greeks.live posted on the X platform that tomorrow will see the largest options settlement of the first quarter of this year, with nearly 40% of options expiring. 1. Key Settlement Data: BTC's Max Pain: Currently pegged to $75,000, this seems unlikely given the current situation. Put/C Ratio: Remaining around 0.6, relatively low, indicating low trading volume for put options, but overall sentiment remains bearish. 2. Volatility (IV) Warning: An IV Crush is imminent. Currently, front-end implied volatility nearing expiration remains strong. However, after tomorrow's settlement, a sharp IV Crush (volatility plunge) is highly probable. Buyers of short-term options are at a significant disadvantage (severe Theta loss), while sellers have a clear advantage. 3. Whales and Block Trades: Today's block trade data on Deribit shows that smart money is aggressively rolling over options. Many institutions closed out their positions expiring tomorrow and built up large positions in June and September out-of-the-money call options (OTM Calls).

data.title: X lost its lawsuit accusing advertisers of boycotting the platform.
data.description: According to market sources, Elon Musk's X platform lost a lawsuit alleging a boycott by advertisers. (Jinshi)

data.title: Euro-Denominated Stablecoins Dominate Non-U.S. Dollar Market
data.description: Euro-denominated stablecoins account for over 80% of the non-U.S. dollar stablecoin market, according to NS3.AI. This segment is valued at approximately $1.2 billion. Over the past three years, the monthly trading volume for non-dollar stablecoins has increased to about $10 billion.

data.title: Musk's X Platform Loses Lawsuit Over Advertiser Boycott Allegations
data.description: Market reports indicate that Elon Musk's X platform has lost a lawsuit concerning allegations of an advertiser boycott. According to Odaily, the legal proceedings were centered around claims that advertisers were intentionally avoiding the platform. The outcome marks a significant development in the ongoing challenges faced by the platform in maintaining its advertising partnerships.

data.title: Resolv Labs Warns Against Trading USR Stablecoin Amid Security Concerns
data.description:
Resolv Labs has issued a strong advisory against trading or purchasing the USR stablecoin. According to ChainCatcher, on-chain data reveals that USR coins, illegally minted by attackers, have been mixed with legitimate USR in various wallets and continue to circulate in secondary markets. The company highlighted that these illicitly minted USR coins lack collateral support and redemption rights, making any transactions potentially involve tainted assets, complicating future asset recovery and management.

Resolv Labs stated that they are in the early stages of evaluating compensation plans for affected users and cannot make any guarantees at this time. They warned that ongoing trading activities might further limit potential compensation options. The team is working closely with relevant protocol partners to develop a solution and plans to release more details in the future.

data.title: U.S. President Donald Trump Announces New Federal Reserve Candidate
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has announced that a highly qualified candidate is set to take over at the Federal Reserve. According to Jin10, this announcement comes as the administration seeks to ensure stability and effective monetary policy management. The identity of the candidate has not been disclosed, but the selection is expected to align with the administration's economic objectives. The Federal Reserve plays a crucial role in shaping the U.S. economy, and the new appointment is anticipated to influence future monetary policies.

data.title: U.S. President Donald Trump Criticizes Fed Chair Powell's Performance
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has expressed strong disapproval of Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell's handling of monetary policy. According to Jin10, Trump described Powell's performance as 'terrible,' reflecting ongoing tensions between the White House and the Federal Reserve. The criticism comes amid debates over interest rates and economic strategies, highlighting the President's dissatisfaction with the central bank's approach. Trump's remarks add to the pressure on Powell, who has been navigating complex economic challenges.
